description: A photograph depicting a rocky, arid landscape under a clear, light blue sky. The image is dominated by large, irregular formations of brown and reddish-brown rock, characteristic of weathered outcrops or volcanic terrain. Sparse, dry, shrub-like vegetation with thin branches and greyish-green foliage grows among the rocks, particularly in the lower centre and foreground. The ground is composed of light brown, dry earth and smaller rock debris. This image illustrates the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) land cover category "6.3.1.0 Bare Rocks, outcrops, cliffs" and is identified as Etna's volcanic landscape in Italy, credited to A. Utanda.
